results.rendered_html:
1. amarṣa (3. a + marṣa) m.
1〉 das Nichtdulden, Nichtleiden, Nichtzugeben  P. 3,3,145 .  VOP. 25,11 .
— 2〉 das Ungehaltensein, Unmuth, Aerger, Zorn  AK. 1,1,7,26 . 3,4,211 .  H. 320 . 321 .  P. 1,4,37 , Sch.  BHAG. 12,15 .  R. 3,33,14 . 4,8,30 . 39 . 40 .  KATHĀS. 10,61 . gato 'smyantamamarṣasya  R. 6,100,3 . sthirāmarṣau 91,6 . sāmarṣa adj.: sāmarṣe hṛdaye  PAÑCAT. III, 264 . buddhirmuneḥ samutpannā . sāmarṣā  VIŚV. 13,11 . sāmarṣam adv.  MṚCCH. 19,17 . sāmarṣatā Aerger, Zorn  RAGH. 7,41 .

results.rendered_html:
amarṣa a. Not enduring or bearing.
— rṣaḥ
1 Non-endurance, tolerance, impatience; amarṣaśūnyena janasya jatunā na jātahārdena na vidviṣādaraḥ  Ki. 1. 33 ; jealousy, jealous anger; kiṃ nu bhavatastātapratāpotkarṣepyamarṣaḥ  U. 5.  In Rhet. amarṣa is one of the 33 minor feelings or vyabhicāribhāva See  S. D. ;  R. G.  thus defines it: parakṛtāvajñādinānāparādhajanyo maunavākpāruṣyādikāraṇabhūtaścittavṛttiviśeṣo' marṣaḥ.
2 Anger, passion, wrath; putravadhāmarṣoddīpitena gāṃḍīvinā  Ve. 2 ; sāmarṣa
(pb) angry, indignant; sāmarṣaṃ angrily.
3 Impetuosity, violence.
4 Determination of purpose.
Comp.
— ja a. arising from anger or impatience.
— hāsaḥ an angry laugh, sarcastic sneer.
